questions.text: Have you ever worked with a therapist, coach, or counselor — and if so, what was that experience like? I'm curious whether you've found professional support helpful for processing things, or if it's just not your thing. If you have worked with someone, any qualities that made them effective (or not)?

When it comes to your presence online — you mentioned interest in the tech/AI space — are you more interested in building an audience/community, or is it more about reputation and professional visibility for career stuff? Do you actually want to be a 'content creator' or is the goal just to be known as someone solid in your field?

How do you handle it when you and someone close to you have a real disagreement — like a friendship conflict, a misunderstanding with family, or tension with a partner? Are you someone who addresses it head-on and ASAP, needs time to cool off first, tries to avoid confrontation entirely, or does it depend on the situation?

What's your relationship with risk and trying new things? Like, when you face a big decision — quitting a job, moving somewhere new, betting on yourself on a project — do you usually lean toward caution or go for it? How do you typically weigh the upside vs. downside?

Do you ever create things just for the joy of it — like write, draw, build projects that aren't about productivity or work, make music, journal, or anything where the output is more important than the outcome? Or is your creative energy mostly channeled into building things that serve a purpose?
